page.content: In the Procter & Gamble Fiscal Year 2024 report, the Omnichannel conversion rates reveal that the E-commerce channel has a conversion rate of 15.2%, with a market share of 25.4%, while the Mobile App channel shows a conversion rate of 12.8% and a market share of 20.3%.

lines.text: In the Procter & Gamble Fiscal Year 2024 report, the Omnichannel conversion rates reveal that the E-commerce channel has a
conversion rate of 15.2%, with a market share of 25.4%, while the Mobile App channel shows a conversion rate of 12.8% and a

page.content: Social Media reflects a conversion rate of 10.5% and a market share of 18.7%, whereas In-Store channels demonstrate an 8.9% conversion rate with a market share of 30.2%.

lines.text: Social Media reflects a conversion rate of 10.5% and a market share of 18.7%, whereas In-Store channels demonstrate an 8.9%
conversion rate with a market share of 30.2%.

page.content: The total conversion rate across all channels stands at 11.6%, with an overall growth of 23.1% and a year-over-year change of 5.4%. In Table 2, the E-commerce metrics for the Asia-Pacific region indicate a penetration rate of 20% for 2024, with growth rates of 15% in India, 30% in China, 5% in Japan, 10% in Australia, and 15% in New Zealand, yielding market shares of 25% in South Korea, while sales growth indicates 1.5 trillion INR in retail sales and 500 billion INR in online sales. For the previous year, 2023, the penetration rate was 18%, with corresponding values of 12% for India, 28% for China, 4% for Japan, 8% for Australia, and 12% for New Zealand, leading to a market share of 20% in South Korea, with a gross margin of 1.4 trillion INR and 480 billion INR in the total market size.
